folio.label: 192v
folio.l: That it was péme , and krew witú blisful steuene
The Sonne he seide , is clomben vp on heuene
Fourty degrees and oon , and more I wys
Madame Pertelote , my worldlis blys
Herkenetú theise blisful bryddes , how they synge
And se the fresshe floures , how they sprynge
Ful is myn herte , of reuel and solas
But sodeynly úi~ fel , a sorweful caas
For euäe the lattere ende , of ioye , is woo
God woot æ worldly ioye , is sone a go
And if a Rethor , coude faire endite´
He in a cronycle saufly , mygút it wryte
As for a souereyn , notabilitee
Now euäy wys man , lat úi~ herkne me
This storie is also trewe , I vndertake

note.notetxt: 391 There is a small cross in the left margin. RJ

folio.l: As is the book , of launcelot de lake
That wo~men holde , in ful gret reuerence
Now wol I turne a geyn , to my sentence
¢ A Colfox ful , of sly iniquytee
That in the groue had woned , ©eres three
By hey ymaginaciou~ , fore cast
The same nygút , thurgú out the hegges brast
In to the ©erd , there Chaunteclere the faire
Was wont , and eke his wyues to repaire
And in a bed of wortes , stille he lay
Til it was passed , vnderne of the day
Waytyngö his tyme , on Chauntecleer to falle
As gladly doon , theise homycides alle
That in a wayt liggen , to murdre men
O false mordrour , liggyngö in thy den
O newe scaryot , newe Gaenylonö
Fals dissimilour , o greek Synonö
That brougútest Troye , al vttirly to sorwe
O Chaunteclere , accursed be the morwe
That æè in to æ ©erd , flaugú , fro the beemes
Thow were ful wel I warned , by thy dreemes
That thilke day , was perilous to thee
But what æ god forwoot , mot nedes bee
After the opynyoun , of certeyn Clerkes
Wytnesse oon úi~ , æ any parfyt Clerk is
That in Scole , is gret altricacioun
In this matere , and gret disputisioun
And hatú ben , of an hundred thousand men
